heading: Fully Supported Modules
body:
Atlas Functions fully support the following built-in modules:

- [assert](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/assert.html)

- [buffer](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/buffer.html)

- [events](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/events.html)

- [net](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/net.html)

- [os](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/os.html)

- [path](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/path.html)

- [punycode](https://github.com/bestiejs/punycode.js)

  > **Note:** The [built-in punycode module is deprecated](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/punycode.html). However, Atlas Functions
  > provide the [punycode](https://www.npmjs.com/package/punycode) module from `npm`
  > automatically. You can import the module with:
  >
  > ```javascript
  > const punycode = require("punycode");
  > ```

- [querystring](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/querystring.html)

- [stream](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/stream.html)

- [string\_decoder](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/string_decoder.html)

- [timers](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/timers.html)

- [tls](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/tls.html)

- [tty](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/tty.html)

- [url](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/url.html)

- [zlib](https://nodejs.org/docs/v10.18.1/api/zlib.html)
